growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Defined saltwater liquid medium + 10 mM D-glucose

up.method: not quantified in text; glucose was NOT depleted in the isolate culture even at the higher HRT (10 mM supplied)

sec.method: qualitative; increased acetate secretion under metabolic stress reported (SI Appendix Fig. S3), no numeric rate given

growth_records.cite: Ugwuodo, Chika Jude et al. Aromatic amino acid metabolism and active transport regulation are implicated in microbial persistence in fractured shale reservoirs. ISME Communications (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismeco/ycae149. [PMC11637423]
growth_records.snippet: This strain was cultivated at 40C in chemostat bioreactors ... comprising NH4Cl (1 g l-1), MgCl2.6H2O (10 g l-1) ... amended with D-Glucose (10 mM) ... The reactor was operated under three HRTs-19.2, 24, and 48 h ... anaerobic conditions ..
growth_records.notes: Metaproteomics/exometabolomics study; no directly measured q-rates or gDW biomass reported in main text. Growth rate DERIVED from chemostat dilution rate: for a well-mixed CSTR D = 1/HRT, and at steady state mu = D. HRT 19.2 h -> D = 1/19.2 = 0.0521 h-1; doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.0521 = 13.3 h. Isolate tested at 3 salinities (70/130/200 g/L NaCl); per-salinity rates not resolved in text so a single record given for this HRT. Glucose (10 mM) not depleted in isolate at higher HRT. Acetate secretion qualitative (SI Fig S3).

growth_records.medium: Defined saltwater liquid medium + 10 mM D-glucose

up.method: not quantified in text (10 mM supplied)

sec.method: qualitative increased secretion under stress (SI Fig. S3)

growth_records.cite: Ugwuodo, Chika Jude et al. Aromatic amino acid metabolism and active transport regulation are implicated in microbial persistence in fractured shale reservoirs. ISME Communications (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismeco/ycae149. [PMC11637423]
growth_records.snippet: The reactor was operated under three HRTs-19.2, 24, and 48 h ... maintained in anaerobic conditions ... pH was maintained at 7.0 ... The density (OD600nm) of the medium was monitored daily until steady state was attained (5-8 days).
growth_records.notes: Growth rate DERIVED from chemostat dilution rate: D = 1/HRT, mu = D at steady state. HRT 24 h -> D = 1/24 = 0.0417 h-1; doubling time = ln2/0.0417 = 16.6 h. No directly measured q-rates in main text.

growth_records.medium: Defined saltwater liquid medium + 10 mM D-glucose

up.method: not quantified; glucose NOT depleted in isolate at this higher HRT

sec.method: qualitative increased secretion under low-flow metabolic stress (SI Fig. S3)

growth_records.cite: Ugwuodo, Chika Jude et al. Aromatic amino acid metabolism and active transport regulation are implicated in microbial persistence in fractured shale reservoirs. ISME Communications (2024). DOI:10.1093/ismeco/ycae149. [PMC11637423]
growth_records.snippet: operated under three HRTs-19.2, 24, and 48 h ... glucose was depleted under the higher HRT in only the PFE reactor, and not the isolate culture ... within the optimal HRT range (when specific growth rate is at its maximum), exponential grow
growth_records.notes: Growth rate DERIVED from chemostat dilution rate: D = 1/HRT, mu = D at steady state. HRT 48 h -> D = 1/48 = 0.0208 h-1; doubling time = ln2/0.0208 = 33.3 h. Glucose not depleted at this HRT in isolate. No numeric q-rates in main text.
